deep_dive.title: Introduction to Stochastic Differential Equations for Generative Machine Learning: A Variational Perspective
deep_dive.abstract: The use of ordinary and stochastic differential equations has led to substantial progress in generative machine learning with applications to, for example, image, video and biomolecule generation. This paper provides a self-contained and informal introduction to the differential equations, the probabilistic framework for using them in generative modeling and the Fokker--Planck equation that governs the temporal evolution of the marginal distribution of the stochastic variables of the differential equations. The variational lower bound on the log-likelihood (the evidence lower bound, ELBO) is derived and used as a general starting point for a discussion of diffusion models, score matching, and flow matching. All of these approaches may be viewed as specific parameterizations of the most general variational approach. A one-dimensional density modeling problem is used as a simple example to compare different parameterizations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: stochastic differential equations for generative modeling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 随机微分方程用于生成建模
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 随机微分方程用于生成建模
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 随机微分方程用于生成建模
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Tutorial on SDEs for generative ML, covering diffusion models, score matching, flow matching, and variational lower bound.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该教程系统介绍了随机微分方程在生成式机器学习中的应用，包括扩散模型、分数匹配和流匹配。从变分角度推导了证据下界，并讨论了Fokker-Planck方程在边际分布演化中的作用。内容自包含，为理解随机桥过程提供了基础。

deep_dive.title: Effective dynamics of the Sinkhorn algorithm in the regime of low entropy regularization
deep_dive.abstract: The Sinkhorn algorithm is the de facto standard method for numerically solving entropy-regularized optimal transport problems over finite sets. In this work, we investigate a phenomenon arising when Sinkhorn is applied with a small regularization parameter $τ$: the evolution of the dual variables (the logarithm of the scaling factors) is approximately piecewise-linear, while the primal variables (the approximate transport plans) exhibit a saddle-to-saddle type behavior. We prove that as $τ\to 0$, the Sinkhorn iterates indeed converge to a continuous-time curve consistent with these observations, when time is rescaled as $t = τk$, and we characterize the limiting "cold Sinkhorn" dynamics explicitly. In particular, we show that it acts as a dual optimization dynamics for the unregularized problem with properties analogous to the simplex algorithm. Notably, this dynamics converges in finite time to an unregularized solution, implying a novel guarantee for the Sinkhorn algorithm itself: it achieves $\tilde{O}(τ)$ dual suboptimality in $k = O(τ^{-1})$ iterations, instead of $k = O(τ^{-2})$ as existing analyses would suggest.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes Sinkhorn dynamics under low entropy regularization, revealing piecewise-linear dual evolution and saddle-to-saddle primal behavior, crucial for OT-based bridge methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究了低熵正则化下Sinkhorn算法的有效动力学。随着正则化参数趋于零，Sinkhorn迭代收敛到一条连续时间曲线，展示了双变量的分段线性演化和原始变量的鞍点行为。这一发现对理解熵正则化最优传输的底层机制至关重要，并为扩散桥等应用提供了理论支撑。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文研究了低熵正则化下Sinkhorn算法的有效动力学。随着正则化参数趋于零，Sinkhorn迭代收敛到一条连续时间曲线，展示了双变量的分段线性演化和原始变量的鞍点行为。这一发现对理解熵正则化最优传输的底层机制至关重要，并为扩散桥等应用提供了理论支撑。

quick_skim.title: A Mathematical Introduction to Diffusion Models
quick_skim.abstract: These notes give a proof-oriented introduction to diffusion models from the viewpoint of sampling, tracing a single arc from classical sampling dynamics to modern diffusion samplers, their error analysis, and inference-time control. Throughout, the material is layered into core definitions and identities proved in full, representative estimates proved under simplifying assumptions, and research-level theorems stated with a proof roadmap. The intended audience is beginning graduate students with a background in probability but no prior exposure to stochastic differential equations, stochastic numerics, or diffusion models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: mathematical introduction to diffusion models for generative sampling bridges
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散模型数学导论，涵盖生成采样桥
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 扩散模型数学导论，涵盖生成采样桥
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 扩散模型数学导论，涵盖生成采样桥
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A proof-oriented tutorial on diffusion models covering classical to modern samplers, error analysis, and inference-time control, providing foundational knowledge for score-based diffusion bridge methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文以证明为导向，从经典采样动力学出发，系统介绍了扩散模型的数学基础，包括扩散采样器、误差分析与推理时控制。内容覆盖核心定义与恒等式、代表性估计以及前沿定理，适合具有概率背景但未接触随机微分方程的研究生。该导论为基于分数的扩散桥方法（如连接两个概率分布的扩散桥）提供了坚实的数学支撑。

quick_skim.title: Highly Data Parallelizable Estimation of the Sliced-Wasserstein Distance Using Cumulative Distribution Functions
quick_skim.abstract: The Sliced Wasserstein (SW) distance has emerged as a computationally attractive alternative to the Wasserstein distance by leveraging one-dimensional optimal transport along random projections. Standard estimators of the SW distance rely on Monte Carlo averages of one-dimensional Wasserstein distances computed via quantile functions, which require sorting projected samples and access to full datasets. In this work, we introduce a new class of estimators for the Sliced Wasserstein distance based on cumulative distribution functions (CDFs) of projected measures, that avoid sorting and scale via massive dataset parallelism. This class includes several estimators, some of them being indexed by hyperparameters controlling their variance or smoothness. We show that they are especially well suited to scenarios in which CDFs are more tractable than quantile functions, such as mixtures of Gaussians, and moreover that they are also naturally compatible with federated learning, since CDFs of projected data can be computed and aggregated locally without requiring the exchange of raw samples.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: efficient estimation of Wasserstein distance, a key tool in optimal transport for diffusion bridges
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 高效估计Wasserstein距离，是扩散桥中最优传输的关键工具
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 高效估计Wasserstein距离，是扩散桥中最优传输的关键工具
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 高效估计Wasserstein距离，是扩散桥中最优传输的关键工具
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CDF-based estimators for Sliced-Wasserstein distance that avoid sorting and enable massive parallelism, benefiting optimal transport applications.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对切片Wasserstein距离标准估计需排序且无法充分利用并行计算的问题，提出基于累积分布函数的新估计器，避免排序并支持大规模数据并行。该方法可显著加速最优传输距离的计算，对扩散桥中的分布匹配有重要价值。实验证明新估计器保持精度同时提高效率。

quick_skim.title: ITSPACE: Monotone Gaussian Optimal Transport Updates
quick_skim.abstract: Covariance matrices serve as compact descriptors of feature distributions in many machine-learning pipelines, including domain adaptation and Gaussian embeddings. Under a centered Gaussian approximation, the unregularized Wasserstein-2 optimal-transport (OT) discrepancy admits a closed form on covariances given by the Bures-Wasserstein (BW) objective on the symmetric positive definite (SPD) cone. We propose ITSPACE (Iterative Transport for Stable Proximal Alignment of Covariance Embeddings), a proximal majorization-minimization method that directly optimizes this exact BW objective through closed-form updates in a square-root factorization. In exact arithmetic, each iteration satisfies a sufficient-decrease inequality for the BW objective; under inexact polar computations, we provide an explicit certificate-gap bound controlling deviations from exact descent. The resulting iterations preserve PSD structure by construction and naturally support rank-restricted factors, making ITSPACE well-suited as a lightweight inner-loop primitive in settings where adaptation must be performed from unlabeled target batches under strict step and compute budgets. Across real-world covariance-alignment benchmarks, ITSPACE reaches low-BW-gap solutions substantially faster than BW-gradient descent, methods based on other covariance geometries, and entropically regularized sample-OT baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimal transport for covariance matrices, relevant to OT in bridges
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 协方差矩阵的最优传输，与桥中的最优传输概念相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 协方差矩阵的最优传输，与桥中的最优传输概念相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 协方差矩阵的最优传输，与桥中的最优传输概念相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ITSPACE optimizes exact Bures-Wasserstein objective for covariance matrices via proximal majorization-minimization, providing a practical OT tool transferable to diffusion bridge settings.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对协方差矩阵提出ITSPACE算法，直接优化Bures-Wasserstein目标函数，通过近端majorization-minimization和平方根分解实现闭式更新。该工作在精确算术中保证目标函数的充分下降，为最优传输在扩散桥中的应用提供了可迁移的方法，尤其适用于需要协方差对齐的桥接场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对协方差矩阵提出ITSPACE算法，直接优化Bures-Wasserstein目标函数，通过近端majorization-minimization和平方根分解实现闭式更新。该工作在精确算术中保证目标函数的充分下降，为最优传输在扩散桥中的应用提供了可迁移的方法，尤其适用于需要协方差对齐的桥接场景。
